overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a presence on Mastodon, LinkedIn, and Bluesky, focusing on research related to social media, disinformation, and computational social science. His largest audience is on LinkedIn, while Bluesky sees higher engagement on a per-post basis due to the audience size. The overall engagement is strong, reflecting his active participation and sharing of relevant content.

contentThemes.description: Sharing updates on research papers, grants, and project milestones.
Discussions and insights related to computational methods for social media analysis.
Focusing on coordinated inauthentic behavior, information operations, and detection methods.
Advertising postdoctoral and PhD research positions.

recommendations: Cross-promote content across platforms to maximize reach and engagement.
Use more visuals (images, videos) to increase post visibility and engagement.
Actively participate in discussions and respond to comments to foster a community.
Experiment with different posting times to identify peak engagement periods on each platform.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky shows good engagement, particularly with research announcements and opportunities. Focus on sharing new research findings and interactive posts.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon has a smaller audience, engagement is focused on coordinated link sharing and methodological discussions. Maintain focused content for this audience.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn has the largest audience and generates high engagement, especially with announcements about grants and job openings. Focus on professional updates and career opportunities.

topPerformingContent.content: Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.

Now hiring: Two postdoctoral researchers to join our team working on computational methods for social media analysis. Background in data science, computational social science, or related fields preferred.
Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
